name: Herzog–Schönheim Theorem

phase_0.boundary_condition: The Herzog–Schönheim theorem: a complete characterization of when a finite group's subgroup system admits a partition into cosets of equal index.

VINIT.justification: Before any partition or coset structure is imposed, G exists as a bare algebraic object with no distinguished subgroup system.

TANCH.chosen_element: the theorem's conclusion: G admits a partition into cosets ⟺ all subgroups have equal index
TANCH.justification: This is the hermetic seal—the complete boundary condition that closes the entire logical system and determines all valid states.

AFWD.justification: Forward morphism: the natural map that carries the subgroup structure of G into H, preserving the partition property.

AREV.chosen_element: kernel ker(φ) and pullback of subgroups
AREV.justification: Reverse morphism: descent from H back to G via the kernel, undoing the forward map and recovering the original subgroup lattice.

CLINK.chosen_element: composition of successive refinements: H₁ ⊆ H₂ ⊆ H₃ ⊆ G
CLINK.justification: Sequential chaining of subgroup containments; each refinement is a morphism in the lattice, composing to form the full tower.

IMSCRIB.chosen_element: the identity subgroup {e} and the full group G
IMSCRIB.justification: Self-reference: {e} and G are the trivial fixed points of the subgroup lattice; they recognize themselves as boundaries.

FSPLIT.chosen_element: partition of G into cosets of subgroup H: G = H·g₁ ∪ H·g₂ ∪ … ∪ H·gₖ
FSPLIT.justification: Co-multiplication: one group G branches into k disjoint cosets, each of equal size |H|. Splits into [coset-system, index-constraint].

FFUSE.chosen_element: reconstitution of G from its coset partition
FFUSE.justification: Multiplication: the k cosets fuse back into G exactly. FFUSE(FSPLIT(G)) = G. The union of all cosets recovers the original group.

EVALT.chosen_element: all subgroups of G have equal index
EVALT.justification: Affirmative branch: the condition is satisfied; the partition exists and is valid.

EVALF.justification: Negative branch: the condition fails; no valid partition into equal-index cosets exists.

ENGAGR.chosen_element: the case where G is abelian and non-cyclic: both partition-exists and partition-fails simultaneously in different subgroup chains
ENGAGR.justification: Paradice: for certain abelian groups, some subgroup systems admit equal-index partitions while others do not—both states held without global resolution.

IFIX.chosen_element: the proof record: the explicit characterization that G admits a partition ⟺ all subgroups have equal index
IFIX.justification: Permanent append-only fixation: once the theorem is proven, this logical fact is irreversible and becomes canonical reference.

phase_2.split_element: FSPLIT: partition of G into cosets of H
split_outputs: index constraint [G:H] = k (all cosets have equal size)

phase_3.void_description: 00 — G is given but no subgroup partition has been defined; the lattice structure exists but is inert.
phase_3.true_description: 01 — G admits a partition into cosets of equal index; the theorem's condition is satisfied and the partition is valid.
phase_3.false_description: 10 — G does not admit such a partition; subgroups have unequal indices and no valid coset partition exists.
phase_3.both_description: 11 — paradice state: G is abelian and non-cyclic; different subgroup chains simultaneously satisfy and violate the equal-index condition, held without global resolution.

steps.domain_action: uninitialized finite group G, no subgroup structure yet distinguished
the Herzog–Schönheim theorem boundary: G admits partition ⟺ all subgroups have equal index
homomorphism φ: G → H carries subgroup structure forward
composition of subgroup tower H₁ ⊆ H₂ ⊆ … ⊆ G
identity subgroup {e} and full group G as self-referential fixed points
partition G into cosets of subgroup H: G = ⋃ᵢ H·gᵢ (splits into coset-system and index-constraint)
all cosets have equal size |H|; index [G:H] is uniform
union of all cosets reconstitutes G exactly; FFUSE(FSPLIT(G)) = G
kernel ker(φ) and pullback of subgroups; descent from H back to G
refinement chain of successive subgroup containments
subgroups have unequal indices; no valid equal-index partition exists
abelian non-cyclic case: both partition-exists and partition-fails simultaneously in different chains
proof record: the characterization is permanently fixed as canonical theorem

phase_5.compiler_frontend: Group-theoretic proof engine: translates the condition 'all subgroups have equal index' into the existence of a coset partition.
phase_5.ipc_mechanism: Subgroup lattice communication: each subgroup H signals its index [G:H] to the partition validator; cosets communicate membership via the group operation.
phase_5.memory_mechanism: Subgroup lattice store: the complete poset of all subgroups of G, indexed by containment and cardinality; coset representatives stored as a canonical transversal.
phase_5.scheduler_mechanism: Lattice traversal order: depth-first exploration of the subgroup poset, checking index equality at each level before attempting partition.
phase_5.alfs_store: The classification of finite groups with equal-index subgroup systems; canonical examples: cyclic groups, elementary abelian groups, dihedral groups.

phase_6.cycle_cost: One index-equality check per subgroup; O(|Λ(G)|) comparisons where Λ(G) is the subgroup lattice.
phase_6.pre_cycle_state: G with unordered subgroup lattice; no partition structure imposed.
phase_6.post_cycle_state: G with either a valid coset partition (if all indices equal) or a proof that no such partition exists (if indices unequal).
phase_6.delta_s_verdict: ΔS ≈ 0 — The theorem is a pure logical characterization: no information is created or destroyed, only reorganized. The partition (if it exists) is a bijective rearrangement of G's elements into cosets, preserving cardinality and group structure. The proof is deterministic and reversible in principle: given the partition, one can verify the equal-index condition; given the condition, one can construct the partition.

lean_scaffold:
-- IGProtocol scaffold: VINIT → TANCH → AFWD → CLINK → IMSCRIB → FSPLIT → EVALT → FFUSE → AREV → CLINK → EVALF → ENGAGR → IFIX
-- Class: Herzog–Schönheim Theorem
-- Fingerprint: sig=(7,2,3,1)
--   self_ref=False | frobenius_order=1
--   dialetheia_complete=True | period=13
-- Expected tier: O₂
-- FSPLIT/FFUSE pairs: [(5, 7)]

import Imscribing.IGMorphism
import Imscribing.IGFunctor

namespace Imscribing
open Primitives Frobenius IGProtocol
open Dimensionality Topology Relational Polarity Grammar
     Fidelity KineticChar Granularity Criticality Protection Stoichiometry Chirality

-- ── Token → IG field mapping ──────────────────────────────────────────────
--   [0] VINIT     dim    := 𐑼               𐑼 → 𐑡  | initial object — ground of distinction
--   [1] TANCH     top    := 𐑡               𐑼 → 𐑾  | terminal object — connectivity boundary
--   [2] AFWD      rel    := 𐑾               𐑡 → 𐑱  | forward morphism — bidirectional arrow
--   [3] CLINK     fid    := 𐑱               𐑾 → 𐑠  | composition — regime coherence
--   [4] IMSCRIB   gram   := 𐑠               𐑱 → 𐑚  | identity — self-imscription
--   [5] FSPLIT    gran   := 𐑚               𐑚 → 𐑚  | split δ — range decomposition
--   [6] EVALT     crit   := ⊙               𐑚 → 𐑙  | evaluate-true — criticality gate open
--   [7] FFUSE     stoi   := 𐑙               𐑙 → 𐑗  | fuse μ — assembly mode
--   [8] AREV      pol    := 𐑗               𐑙 → 𐑱  | reverse morphism — parity flip
--   [9] CLINK     fid    := 𐑱               𐑗 → 𐑖  | composition — regime coherence
--   [10] EVALF     chir   := 𐑖               𐑱 → 𐑳  | evaluate-false — chirality check
--   [11] ENGAGR    stoi   := 𐑳               𐑖 → 𐑭  | engage paradox — B-state, both arms
--   [12] IFIX      prot   := 𐑭               𐑳 → 𐑼  | irreversible fixation — winding number

-- ── Main IGProtocol term ────────────────────────────────────────────────────

noncomputable def herzog_sch_nheim_theorem_protocol : IGProtocol 𐑼 𐑭 :=
  .withGram 𐑠 <|
  -- Seq chain:
  (.arrow 𐑼 𐑼 𐑡)  -- [0] VINIT | dim := 𐑼 | initial object — ground of distinction (uninitialized finite group G, no subgroup structure yet distinguished)
  (.arrow 𐑡 𐑼 𐑾)  -- [1] TANCH | top := 𐑡 | terminal object — connectivity boundary (the Herzog–Schönheim theorem boundary: G admits partition ⟺ all subgroups hav...)
  (.arrow 𐑾 𐑡 𐑱)  -- [2] AFWD | rel := 𐑾 | forward morphism — bidirectional arrow (homomorphism φ: G → H carries subgroup structure forward)
  (.arrow 𐑱 𐑾 𐑠)  -- [3] CLINK | fid := 𐑱 | composition — regime coherence (composition of subgroup tower H₁ ⊆ H₂ ⊆ … ⊆ G)
  (.arrow 𐑠 𐑱 𐑚)  -- [4] IMSCRIB | gram := 𐑠 | identity — self-imscription (identity subgroup {e} and full group G as self-referential fixed points)
  -- FSPLIT [5] (gran := 𐑚) (partition G into cosets of subgroup H: G = ⋃ᵢ H·gᵢ (splits into coset-system and index-constraint)) / FFUSE [7] (stoi := 𐑙)
  .seq
    (.prod
      -- T-branch (1 nodes)
      (.arrow ⊙ 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [6] EVALT | crit := ⊙ | evaluate-true — criticality gate open (all cosets have equal size |H|; index [G:H] is uniform)
      -- F-branch (0 nodes)
      (.refl 𐑙))  -- F-branch: empty arc (direct to FFUSE.F)
    -- reconnect at FFUSE [7]: μ closes the Frobenius pair
    (.arrow 𐑙 𐑙 𐑗)  -- [7] FFUSE | stoi := 𐑙 (union of all cosets reconstitutes G exactly; FFUSE(FSPLIT(G)) = G)
  (.arrow 𐑗 𐑙 𐑱)  -- [8] AREV | pol := 𐑗 | reverse morphism — parity flip (kernel ker(φ) and pullback of subgroups; descent from H back to G)
  (.arrow 𐑱 𐑗 𐑖)  -- [9] CLINK | fid := 𐑱 | composition — regime coherence (refinement chain of successive subgroup containments)
  (.arrow 𐑖 𐑱 𐑳)  -- [10] EVALF | chir := 𐑖 | evaluate-false — chirality check (subgroups have unequal indices; no valid equal-index partition exists)
  (.arrow 𐑳 𐑖 𐑭)  -- [11] ENGAGR | stoi := 𐑳 | engage paradox — B-state, both arms (abelian non-cyclic case: both partition-exists and partition-fails simultaneo...)
  (.arrow 𐑭 𐑳 𐑼)  -- [12] IFIX | prot := 𐑭 | irreversible fixation — winding number (proof record: the characterization is permanently fixed as canonical theorem)

-- ── Evaluation arm sub-defs ─────────────────────────────────────────────────

-- truth arm
noncomputable def herzog_sch_nheim_theorem_true_arm : IGProtocol 𐑼 𐑭 :=
  (herzog_sch_nheim_theorem_protocol).restrictToEVALT

-- false arm
noncomputable def herzog_sch_nheim_theorem_false_arm : IGProtocol 𐑼 𐑭 :=
  (herzog_sch_nheim_theorem_protocol).restrictToEVALF

-- ── Verification theorems ───────────────────────────────────────────────────

theorem herzog_sch_nheim_theorem_tier : TierFunctor.obj 𐑼 = .O₂ := by decide

-- Frobenius (split → fuse): μ∘δ = id on .prod branch
-- Proof: apply igFrobAlg_self_fusion; exact mu_delta_A_id
-- (requires mu_delta_A_id from IGFunctor library)

end Imscribing
